_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL
name: Nextcloud
description: A suite of client-server software for creating and using file hosting services. It is enterprise-ready with comprehensive features.
startup:
if [ "${DB_TYPE}" = "mysql" ]; then
    export MYSQL_HOST="${DB_HOST}"
    export MYSQL_DATABASE="${DB_NAME}"
    export MYSQL_USER="${DB_USER}"
    export MYSQL_PASSWORD="${DB_PASSWORD}"
elif [ "${DB_TYPE}" = "postgres" ]; then
    export POSTGRES_HOST="${DB_HOST}"
    export POSTGRES_DB="${DB_NAME}"
    export POSTGRES_USER="${DB_USER}"
    export POSTGRES_PASSWORD="${DB_PASSWORD}"
fi

sed -i "s/Listen 80/Listen ${SERVER_PORT}/g" /etc/apache2/ports.conf
sed -i "s/<VirtualHost \*:80>/<VirtualHost \*:${SERVER_PORT}>/g" /etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf
sed -i "s/<VirtualHost \*:80>/<VirtualHost \*:${SERVER_PORT}>/g" /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/000-default.conf

if [ ! -d "/home/container/html" ]; then
    echo "Creating Nextcloud html directory..."
    mkdir -p /home/container/html
fi

if [ -d "/var/www/html" ] && [ ! -L "/var/www/html" ]; then
    if [ "$(ls -A /var/www/html)" ]; then
        echo "Copying original Nextcloud files to persistent storage..."
        cp -a /var/www/html/. /home/container/html/
    fi
    rm -rf /var/www/html
fi
ln -sfT /home/container/html /var/www/html

if [ "$(id -u)" = "0" ]; then
    echo "Running as root. Adjusting permissions..."
    chown -R www-data:www-data /home/container/html
    exec /entrypoint.sh apache2-foreground
else
    echo "Running as non-root user. Configuring Apache to run as current user..."
    sed -i "s/export APACHE_RUN_USER=www-data/export APACHE_RUN_USER=$(whoami)/g" /etc/apache2/envvars
    sed -i "s/export APACHE_RUN_GROUP=www-data/export APACHE_RUN_GROUP=$(id -gn)/g" /etc/apache2/envvars
    exec apache2-foreground
fi

variables.name: Database Type
variables.description: The database engine to use (mysql, postgres, or sqlite). SQLite requires no extra DB parameters.

variables.name: Database Host
variables.description: IP/host and port of the database server (e.g. 10.0.0.5:3306 or 127.0.0.1:5432).

variables.name: Database Name
variables.description: The name of the database to connect to.

variables.name: Nextcloud Trusted Domains
variables.description: Space-separated list of domains/IPs allowed to access Nextcloud.
